name: Password Aging with Long Expiration
description: The product implements password aging but with an excessively long expiration period, giving attackers an extended window to crack compromised password hashes before forced rotation occurs. Note: modern NIST guidance discourages mandatory password expiration entirely, as it often leads to weaker password choices.
detection_pattern: Look for META nodes (password expiration policy, aging configuration) applied to AUTH nodes (authentication system) where the expiration period exceeds reasonable thresholds (e.g., >90 days) without a CONTROL mediator (adaptive expiration, breach-triggered rotation, risk-based policy) that adjusts aging based on threat context.
